summary: Comprehensive testing of LaunchLoop product discovery platform completed. Wrote 22 pytest backend tests covering auth, projects, leaderboard, upvotes, comments, profiles, upload — all 22 passed (100%). Verified frontend home page, leaderboard with period tabs, discover with search/filters, login flow, submit project 3-step form, profile pages. UI loads cleanly with bold typography and seeded demo projects displayed correctly.

minor.issue: 'upvotes' and 'comments' counts reflect only NEW user-created records (db.upvotes / db.comments), not the seeded upvotes_count/comments_count on projects. Home page shows '0 upvotes / 0 comments' even though seeded projects display 142/980/18 etc. Mostly cosmetic — could aggregate from projects to show realistic totals.

issues: Hero 'Stay relevant.' text uses outlined/stroke style which can be hard to read on light background — intentional but worth noting
Leaderboard period tabs use labels 'This week / This month / All time' (not 'Weekly/Monthly/All-time' as stated in spec) — labels are friendly but check spec alignment

action_items: (Optional) Update /api/stats to compute upvotes/comments from project counters so the home page stat panel isn't 0/0 with seed data
(Optional) If spec strictly requires labels 'Weekly/Monthly/All-time', rename Leaderboard tab labels (data-testid period-weekly/monthly/all are already correct)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py JWT cookies use secure=False — acceptable for preview env but must be secure=True in production over HTTPS
set_auth_cookies samesite='lax' is fine for same-site flows; consider 'none' + secure=True for cross-origin frontends
Brute-force lockout (>=5 fails) mentioned in playbook is NOT implemented — POST /api/auth/login has no rate limiting / account lockout
/api/projects/{slug} uses slug as path param but routes are declared in order — /projects/leaderboard correctly precedes /projects/{slug}, OK
Project list does not paginate (limit 50 default, no offset) — fine for now, but will be a scaling issue
annotate_projects does N+1 queries (one db.users lookup per project, plus one upvote lookup) — can become slow; consider $lookup or bulk fetch
/api/files/{path:path} reads bytes through requests sync call inside async endpoint — blocks the event loop; acceptable for small files but should use asyncio.to_thread or async http client
bcrypt hashes verified (admin_session login succeeded — implies hash format $2b$)

CORS allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will reject credentialed responses with wildcard origin. In preview it works because everything is same-origin proxied, but cross-origin clients with cookies will fail. Should set explicit origin list when CORS_ORIGINS is set.
test_credentials: admin@launchloop.dev / admin123 (admin) and demo@launchloop.dev / demo123 (user) — both verified working via login API and UI
seed_data_creation: Seed data was created by app on startup (1 admin, 1 demo user, 8 demo projects). Additionally TEST_-prefixed users/projects created during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 22 tests covering all major flows. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Leaderboard component renders with data-testid='period-weekly|monthly|all' (UI labels: 'This week'/'This month'/'All time'). httpOnly auth cookies (access_token, refresh_token). Home page hero is text-heavy with outlined accent; submit page is 3-step. /api/stats returns 0 for upvotes/comments because db.upvotes/db.comments collections are empty on fresh seed.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Minor stats discrepancy on home page (8 projects, 5 makers, 0 upvotes, 0 comments) is because seed inserts only upvotes_count/comments_count integers on project docs but does not create rows in db.upvotes/db.comments; /api/stats counts those collections. Mitigation: either count_documents projects with upvotes_count or aggregate sum.
